Package com.dtwthalion.mpgo.providers

Interface StorageProvider

All Superinterfaces:
AutoCloseable
All Known Implementing Classes:
Hdf5Provider, MemoryProvider, SqliteProvider
public interface StorageProvider extends AutoCloseable
Storage backend entry point.

Implementations are discovered via ServiceLoader. Each implementation must also expose a no-argument public constructor so the loader can instantiate it; the useful work happens in open(String, Mode).

Implementations are required to support the capability floor listed in docs/format-spec.md — hierarchical groups, typed 1-D datasets, partial reads, chunked storage, compression, compound datasets with VL strings, scalar and array attributes. Unsupported capabilities raise UnsupportedOperationException at the call site.

API status: Stable (Provisional per M39 — may change before v1.0).

Cross-language equivalents: Objective-C MPGOStorageProvider, Python mpeg_o.providers.base.StorageProvider.

Since:
0.6

    Open modes mirroring h5py semantics.

  • Method Details

    • providerName

      String providerName()
      Short identifier used for logging and registry lookup.

    • supportsUrl

      boolean supportsUrl(String pathOrUrl)
      Return true if this provider supports opening the given path or URL. Used by the registry to pick a provider by scheme when the caller hasn't named one explicitly.

    • open

      StorageProvider open(String pathOrUrl, StorageProvider.Mode mode)
      Open the backing store. The provider instance is returned so chaining is possible: new Hdf5Provider().open(...).

      Re-opening an already-open provider is an error.

    • rootGroup

      StorageGroup rootGroup()
      Root group ("/"). Must be called after open(java.lang.String, com.dtwthalion.mpgo.providers.StorageProvider.Mode).

    • isOpen

      boolean isOpen()

    • nativeHandle

      default Object nativeHandle()
      Return the underlying native storage handle — an Hdf5File for Hdf5Provider, null for MemoryProvider.

      Escape hatch for byte-level code (signatures, encryption, native compression filters) that cannot be expressed through the protocol. Any caller that invokes this is pinned to a specific backend.

    • close

      void close()
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
